sections.title: 1.  What is VeilClip?
sections.body:
VeilClip is a clipboard manager for Windows. Think of it like a notebook that automatically writes down everything you copy — text, links, images, and file paths — so you can go back and use any of them again whenever you want.

Normally, your computer only remembers the very last thing you copied. The moment you copy something new, the old thing disappears forever. VeilClip fixes that problem. It quietly sits in the background and saves every single thing you copy, so nothing is ever lost by accident.

VeilClip is completely free to use and always will be. It is open-source, which means anyone can read the program code and check exactly what it does — there are no hidden surprises.

sections.title: 2.  Your Privacy & Security — 100 % Offline
sections.body:
This is the most important thing to know about VeilClip, so please read it carefully.

VeilClip works 100 % offline. It never connects to the internet. It does not send your clipboard data to any server, any company, or any person. Everything you copy is saved only in a small database file that lives on your own computer.

No cloud. No sync. No account required. No tracking. No advertisements. Nobody except you can ever see what you have copied.

You can use VeilClip on a plane, in a cabin with no wifi, or completely disconnected from the internet — it will work exactly the same as always.

VeilClip is open-source. The full code is publicly available for anyone to inspect. What you see is exactly what the program does — nothing is hidden.

VeilClip is free to use. Updates and new features are made possible by people who choose to donate. If VeilClip saves you time, please consider supporting it — every contribution helps keep the project alive. See section 19 for more.

sections.title: 3.  Starting VeilClip
sections.body:
When VeilClip starts, it goes straight to the system tray — the small area near the clock in the bottom-right corner of your screen. You will see a small VeilClip icon there.

VeilClip begins watching your clipboard immediately. Every time you press Ctrl+C to copy something, VeilClip saves it automatically. You do not need to do anything special — it just works quietly in the background.

By default, VeilClip is set to start automatically every time you turn on your computer, so you never have to remember to open it. You can turn this off in Settings under Window Behaviour.

sections.title: 4.  Opening and Closing the Clipboard Panel
sections.body:
The clipboard panel is the main window where you can see everything you have copied.

There are two ways to open it:

Option 1 — Keyboard shortcut: Press Alt + V at the same time. This works from anywhere on your computer, even when you are inside another program. The panel will appear near your mouse cursor.

Option 2 — Tray icon: Click the VeilClip icon in the system tray at the bottom-right corner of the screen near the clock.

To close the panel:
  •  Press the Escape key
  •  Click the X button at the top-right of the panel
  •  Press Alt+V again
  •  Click anywhere outside the panel (if that option is turned on)

The panel remembers its size and position between sessions. You can drag it anywhere on screen by holding the top bar, and resize it by dragging the bottom-right corner.

sections.title: 5.  Using the Clipboard List
sections.body:
When you open the panel, you will see a list of everything you have copied. The most recent item is always at the top.

To copy an item again: Simply click on it. It is instantly copied to your clipboard and you can paste it anywhere with Ctrl+V.

Each item shows a small icon that tells you what type it is:
  📄  Text — a piece of written text
  🔗  Link — a web address (URL)
  🖼  Image — a picture
  📁  File path — the location of a file on your computer

Color preview: If you copy a colour code like #6C63FF or rgb(108, 99, 255), VeilClip shows a small coloured square next to the item so you can see the actual colour at a glance. Clicking the square copies just the colour code.

The item count at the top-right shows how many items are in your history. When you switch to the Favorites tab it shows your favorites count instead.

sections.title: 6.  Searching Your History
sections.body:
If you have many copied items and want to find a specific one quickly, use the search box at the top of the panel.

Just start typing — the list instantly filters to show only items that match what you typed. It searches both the content of the item and the name of the program it came from.

To clear the search and see all items again: press Escape, or click the small X button inside the search box.

Group mode: Click the Group button next to the search box to organise your history by the application each item came from. For example, all text copied from a browser will appear together, and all text from Notepad will appear in its own group.

sections.title: 7.  Pinning Important Items
sections.body:
If there is something you want to keep forever — like your home address, a piece of text you use very often, or a frequently used link — you can pin it so it is always at the top of your list.

To pin an item: Right-click on it and choose Pin, or click the pin button (📌) on the right side of the item card.

Pinned items always appear at the top of the list, above everything else. They are never automatically deleted, even if you have Auto-Delete History turned on. They also survive when you use Clear History.

To unpin: right-click and choose Pin again, or click the pin button again. It works like a toggle.

sections.title: 8.  Deleting Items and Undoing a Delete
sections.body:
To delete a single item: Click the X button on the right side of the item card. The item disappears immediately from the list.

Undo a delete: After deleting, a small notification appears at the top of the panel saying 'Deleted — Undo'. Click the Undo button or press Ctrl+Z on your keyboard to bring the item back. You have 3 seconds to undo before the deletion becomes permanent.

Multiple undo: If you delete several items one after another, you can undo them one by one — up to 8 consecutive deletions can be undone. The notification shows how many deletions are pending.

When you close the panel, all pending deletions are committed immediately and can no longer be undone.

sections.title: 9.  Selecting Multiple Items at Once
sections.body:
You can select several items at the same time to copy or delete them all in one go — much faster than doing them one by one.

To enter Select mode: Click the 'Select' button in the top-right area of the panel header.

Once in Select mode, click on any item to select it. A count of selected items is shown at the top of the panel. Click a selected item again to deselect it.

Copy all selected: Click 'Copy' in the toolbar to combine all selected text items into one and copy them to your clipboard. Images cannot be multi-copied this way.

Delete all selected: Click 'Delete' to remove all selected items at once. You can undo this with Ctrl+Z.

To leave Select mode: Click 'Cancel', or switch tabs.

sections.title: 10.  Editing Text Items
sections.body:
Sometimes you copy something but want to change it slightly before using it — for example removing extra spaces, fixing a typo, or adjusting part of a link.

To edit a text item: Right-click on it and choose 'Edit Text'. A text editor will open where you can change the content however you like. When you click OK, the updated text is saved to your history and automatically copied to your clipboard.

You can also copy an item as plain text: Right-click and choose 'Copy as Plain Text'. This removes any rich formatting (bold, italic, special characters) and copies just the plain words. Useful when pasting into a program that does not accept formatted text.

sections.title: 11.  The Image Editor
sections.body:
VeilClip has a built-in image editor for any image in your clipboard history.

To open it: Right-click on any image item and choose 'Edit Image'.

Inside the image editor you can:
  •  Crop — cut out just the part of the image you want
  •  Rotate — turn the image left or right
  •  Flip — mirror the image horizontally or vertically
  •  Resize — change the dimensions of the image
  •  Adjust brightness, contrast, and other settings

When you save, the result is added to your clipboard history as a new image item, ready to copy and paste.

sections.title: 12.  Favorites — Save What You Use Most
sections.body:
Favorites is a special place where you can save items you want to keep permanently and find quickly, without scrolling through your whole history.

To add an item to Favorites: Right-click on it and hover over 'Add to Favorites'. Choose a category (Work, Personal, Passwords, General) or create a new category of your own.

To view your Favorites: Click the 'Favorites' tab at the top of the panel.

Filter by category: Click a category button at the top of the Favorites tab to show only that category. Click 'All' to see everything.

Images: When you save an image to Favorites, a small thumbnail preview is shown on the card.

Manage categories: Click the 'Manage' button in the Favorites tab to rename or delete your custom categories.

To remove a favorite: Click the X button on its card.

sections.title: 13.  Dragging Items into Other Programs
sections.body:
You can drag items directly from VeilClip into other programs — no need to copy and paste.

For example: drag a link directly into a browser address bar. Drag an image directly into a photo editor, a document, or a folder on your desktop. Drag a file path into a terminal window.

To drag an item: Click and hold the left mouse button on any item card, then move your mouse to where you want to drop it, and release the button.

A small thumbnail of the item follows your mouse while you drag so you can see exactly what you are moving.

sections.title: 14.  Locked Notes — Your PIN-Protected Secret Space
sections.body:
Locked Notes is a hidden space inside VeilClip where you can store sensitive information — passwords, secret codes, private notes, or anything you want to keep safe from other people.

Locked Notes are protected by a PIN that only you know. Nobody can see the contents without the correct PIN — not even someone who has physical access to your computer.

Items saved in Locked Notes never appear in the main clipboard list. They are stored separately and encrypted.

To open Locked Notes: Go to Settings, click 'Locked Notes', then click 'Open Locked Notes'. The first time, you will set a PIN. Every time after that, you will need to enter your PIN.

Important: If you forget your PIN, your locked notes cannot be recovered. There is no password reset. Please choose a PIN you will remember.

sections.title: 15.  Settings — Full Overview
sections.body:
Open Settings by clicking the ⚙ button in the panel header, or right-clicking the tray icon and choosing Settings.

HOTKEY — Change the keyboard shortcut (default Alt+V). Click Change, press your new combination. Use 'Reset to Default' to go back to Alt+V.

WINDOW BEHAVIOUR:
  •  Always open at cursor — panel appears at mouse position
  •  Close after copying — panel closes when you click an item
  •  Always on top — panel stays in front of all other windows
  •  Close on outside click — clicking outside closes the panel
  •  Run at Windows startup — starts with your computer

HISTORY — Auto-delete old items after a chosen number of hours. Pinned items are always kept.

STORAGE — See the database file location. Open the folder, copy the path, or move the database to a different drive.

BACKUP — Automatically back up your database on a schedule to any folder you choose (see section 16).

EXPORT / IMPORT — Save history to a file or load it back (see section 16).

LOCKED NOTES — Open the PIN-protected vault (see section 14).

PRIVACY — Clear history, clear pinned items, or Reset Everything to wipe all data and start fresh (see section 17).

sections.title: 16.  Backup and Export — Keep Your Data Safe
sections.body:
VeilClip gives you two ways to make sure your clipboard history is never lost.

AUTOMATIC BACKUP
Go to Settings → Backup. Choose a folder on your computer or an external drive. Set how often to back up (e.g. every 24 hours) and how many copies to keep (e.g. the last 7). VeilClip will then back up automatically without any action from you. Click 'Back Up Now' to create a backup immediately at any time.

EXPORT TO FILE
Go to Settings → Export. Choose JSON (best for importing back into VeilClip) or CSV (opens in Excel or Google Sheets). A copy of your full clipboard history is saved to the file you choose.

IMPORT FROM FILE
Load a previously exported file back into VeilClip. The items are merged into your current history and any duplicates are skipped automatically.

sections.title: 17.  Reset Everything — Starting Completely Fresh
sections.body:
If you want to completely wipe VeilClip and start from scratch — as if you had just installed it for the first time — use Reset Everything.

To use it: Go to Settings → Privacy → Reset Everything. A confirmation window will appear. Click 'Yes, Reset Everything'.

What gets deleted:
  •  All clipboard history (including pinned items)
  •  All favorites
  •  All locked notes
  •  All settings and preferences
  •  All temporary files

After the reset, VeilClip restarts automatically and opens exactly as it does on a brand new installation — the welcome message appears and the panel opens.

Warning: This cannot be undone. Export or back up anything important before using this option.

sections.title: 18.  The Tray Icon and Its Menu
sections.body:
The VeilClip icon lives in the system tray at the bottom-right corner of your screen, near the clock.

Left-click or double-click the icon to open the clipboard panel.

Right-click the icon for a quick menu:
  Open — opens the clipboard panel
  Settings — opens the Settings window
  Clear History — deletes all non-pinned items
  Donate — opens the donation window
  Help — opens this help window
  Exit — closes VeilClip completely

When you choose Exit, your history is saved and will still be there the next time you start VeilClip.

sections.title: 19.  Free Forever — Kept Alive by Your Support
sections.body:
VeilClip is completely free to use and will always remain free. There is no paid version, no subscription, and no features locked behind a paywall.

VeilClip is developed and maintained by independent developers who work on it in their spare time. There are no big company budgets — just real people building something genuinely useful for everyone.

New features, bug fixes, and updates for future versions of Windows are made possible entirely by donations from users like you. When you donate, you are directly funding the next improvement to VeilClip.

How to donate:
  •  Click the 💜 button in the panel header
  •  Right-click the tray icon and choose Donate
  •  Buy Me a Coffee (card / PayPal) or cryptocurrency (Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDT, Solana, and many more)

Even the smallest donation makes a real difference and is deeply appreciated. If VeilClip saves you time every day, please consider giving something back — it helps keep this project alive and growing for everyone.

Thank you for using VeilClip.

sections.title: 20.  Quick Tips & Keyboard Shortcuts
sections.body:
The most useful shortcuts and tips:

Alt+V — open or close the panel from anywhere
Escape — close the panel (or clear the search if text is typed)
Ctrl+Z — undo the last item deletion (within 3 seconds)
Click an item — copy it to clipboard instantly
Right-click an item — edit, pin, add to favorites, delete
Drag an item — drop it directly into another program

Tip: Use 'Select' mode to delete many old items at once — much faster than one by one.

Tip: Save things you type often (your email address, a code snippet, a standard reply) to Favorites for instant one-click access, no matter how long your history gets.

Tip: Store passwords and private codes in Locked Notes — they never appear in the main clipboard list and are always PIN-protected.

Tip: Set up automatic backups to an external drive so your history is safe even if something happens to your computer.

Tip: You can resize the panel by dragging its bottom-right corner. The size and position are remembered next time you open it.
